response.justification: Bogdan Szabo has a broad web and full-stack engineering background with consistent software craftsmanship practices, but falls critically short on the role's core requirements. The minimum qualification of 8 years C++ experience is essentially unmet — C++ appears only in a bachelor thesis project and a brief ~6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015, and is absent from his listed skills entirely. There is no formal technical leadership track record for cloud or platform engineering, and his infrastructure exposure (AWS CDK for a pricing service, hobbyist-scale Kubernetes for GISCollective) is insufficiently deep for leading an internal cloud compute platform. The profile is well-suited to senior product or application engineering roles, not a platform leadership position of this caliber.
strengths: Consistent application of software craftsmanship practices (TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD) across multiple roles and stacks
Hands-on distributed systems exposure at Sociomantic, handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency async I/O
Demonstrated ability to own and deliver end-to-end projects independently, including an open-source GIS platform deployed on Kubernetes
concerns: C++ experience is negligible — a 6-month NI engagement and a bachelor thesis project do not approach the required 8-year minimum, which is a hard disqualifier
No formal technical lead or staff-level platform leadership title; community workshop facilitation is not equivalent to organizational team mentoring at scale
Cloud infrastructure depth is shallow — AWS CDK usage is application-layer (pricing configs), not compute platform architecture; no evidence of storage, networking, or hardware-layer ownership
